# Noto Now

> Noto Now is a bilingual (English / Japanese) editorial site presenting the NOW Institute's architecture and urban-recovery framework for Japan's Noto Peninsula (Oku-Noto), developed in partnership with the City of Wajima following the magnitude 7.6 earthquake of January 1, 2024. The project pairs disaster-resistant spatial planning with the region's cultural and economic engines — Wajima-nuri lacquerware, Kiriko festivals, agriculture, seafood, and Wagyu.

The site is a static, five-page bilingual site. Every page contains both English and Japanese content; a client-side toggle switches languages without changing the URL, so each page has a single canonical URL.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.notonow.org/): Overview of the project — the disaster, the region's cultural economic engines, urban strategies, architectural speculations, and field documentation.
- [About](https://www.notonow.org/about): About the project and its partners, and the "Noto Now 1" narrative — the disaster, the strategy, the approach, and the goal.
- [History](https://www.notonow.org/history): Oku-Noto's heritage crafts and industries, the 2024 earthquake damage, and a gallery of on-the-ground site images.
- [Design](https://www.notonow.org/design): The planning framework — three scales of design, four regional strategies for Ishikawa, urban strategies for Wajima, the Suzu disaster response plan, and architectural speculations.
- [Credits](https://www.notonow.org/credits): Publishing and contributor credits.

## Partners

- The NOW Institute
- City of Wajima
- USC School of Architecture
- Rise-Kyoto
- Nippon Design Center USA
- University of Hiroshima
